observe: One core, one thing at a time — no matter how fast it switches, it's still one thing.
fault: You called that parallel. It wasn't. Look again at what's actually running at once.
repair: Compare the layouts. Where does true simultaneity actually happen?
escape: Pick the real parallel path — you won't get a second look.

observe: Systems vary from single-core to multiprocessor and multicore organization.
fault: A workload was assumed parallel but is running on a single execution unit.
repair: Compare single-core, multiprocessor, and multicore layouts.
escape: Select the configuration offering true parallel execution.

hints: Count the actual independent execution units, not the number of running tasks.
